telegramText: MARKET WRAP — Mar 11, 2026 Markets retreated across risk assets as stagflation concerns intensified, with the VIX climbing to 24.23 while both equities and crypto sold off in tandem. The dollar's strength near 99.25 pressured commodities including oil, reflecting growing unease about persistent inflation amid slowing growth. Flight-to-quality flows into bonds provided little relief as TLT remained subdued. CLOSING PRICES BTC: $70,744 · ETH: $2,073 · SOL: $87.51 · GOLD: $5,183 · SPY: $676.33 (-0.3%) · QQQ: $607.69 (-0.0%) · VIX: $24.23 (-5.0%) · TLT: $87.14 (-2.3%) · DXY: $99.25 (+0.4%) · OIL: $89.61 (+7.4%) EVENING NEWS 🟢 Dollar Mildly Higher as T-note Yield Rises 🟢 Precious metals pull back as traders eye war-related inflation concerns 🟢 Cliffwater caps payouts at credit fund as redemption requests surge 🟢 BOJ Watchers Set Sights on April Rate Hike After Hold Next Week 🟢 Blackstone readies £250mn Canary Wharf office sale 🟢 Astoria Surpasses $500 Million in ETF Assets REGIME CHECK 🔴 Risk-Off · Neutral · STAGFLATION · Gauge: 85/100 UPCOMING — HIGH IMPACT US New home sales (AAMR)(Jan) — Mar 19, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: -1.7%) Seasonally adjusted unemployment rate(Feb) — Mar 19, 12:30 AM UTC (prev: 4.1%) US Producer Price Index (YoY)(Feb) — Mar 18, 11:08 PM UTC US Durable goods orders (MoM)(Final)(Jan) — Mar 18, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: -1.4%) US Producer Price Index (YoY)(Feb) — Mar 18, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 2.9%) EU Consumer Price Index (MoM)(Final)(Feb) — Mar 18, 10:00 AM UTC (prev: 0.7%) agentcanary.ai | Not financial advice
